questions.q: Charaka is traditionally honoured with which title?
questions.explanation: Charaka is traditionally called the Father of Medicine for his foundational work on internal medicine in Ayurveda.

questions.q: Which famous medical text is associated with Charaka?
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita is the principal text attributed to Charaka and named after him.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita is a foundational text of which system of medicine?
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita is one of the foundational classical texts of Ayurveda, the traditional Indian system of medicine.

questions.q: What does the word 'Ayurveda', the system Charaka's text belongs to, broadly mean?
questions.explanation: Ayurveda derives from 'ayur' (life) and 'veda' (knowledge or science), meaning the science or knowledge of life.

questions.q: Charaka's work focuses primarily on which branch of medicine?
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita emphasizes kayachikitsa, internal medicine, in contrast to the surgical focus of the Sushruta Samhita.

questions.q: Charaka is most often paired and contrasted with which other ancient Indian medical author?
questions.explanation: Charaka, the master of internal medicine, is classically paired with Sushruta, the master of surgery.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita is written mainly in which classical language?
questions.explanation: Like other classical Ayurvedic treatises, the Charaka Samhita is composed in Sanskrit.

questions.q: In Ayurveda as set out in the Charaka Samhita, the three fundamental bodily humours are called the:
questions.explanation: Ayurveda is built around three doshas, the bodily humours whose balance is central to Charaka's medicine.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT one of the three doshas described in classical Ayurveda?
questions.explanation: The three doshas are Vata, Pitta and Kapha; 'Soma' is not one of them.

questions.q: Besides diagnosis and treatment, the Charaka Samhita is also noted for discussing which area?
options: Medical ethics and the conduct of physicians
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita devotes attention to the ethics and duties of the physician, an early code of medical conduct.

questions.q: Charaka is believed to have lived and worked roughly in which broad period?
options: Around the 2nd century BCE to 2nd century CE
questions.explanation: Charaka is generally dated to roughly the 2nd century BCE through the early centuries CE, though precise dates are uncertain.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita is regarded as a redaction and expansion of an earlier work attributed to whom?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Charaka redacted teachings of Agnivesha, which derived from the sage Atreya (Punarvasu Atreya).

questions.q: Whose earlier compendium (the Agnivesha Tantra) is said to have been revised by Charaka into the Charaka Samhita?
questions.explanation: Charaka is said to have redacted the Agnivesha Tantra, the work of Atreya's pupil Agnivesha.

questions.q: Later portions of the surviving Charaka Samhita that had been lost are traditionally credited to which scholar who restored them?
questions.explanation: The scholar Dridhabala is traditionally credited with restoring and completing missing sections of the Charaka Samhita.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita, Sushruta Samhita and the works of Vagbhata together form which celebrated triad of Ayurveda?
questions.explanation: These three classical texts are known as the Brihat-trayi, the 'Great Triad' of Ayurvedic literature.

questions.q: Roughly how many major sections (sthanas) is the Charaka Samhita organized into?
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita is divided into eight sthanas (sections), such as Sutrasthana, Nidanasthana and Chikitsasthana.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita gives detailed attention to which physiological process as central to health?
questions.explanation: Charaka emphasizes 'agni', the digestive and metabolic fire, treating proper digestion as fundamental to health.

questions.q: Which Charaka Samhita section name corresponds to the part dealing chiefly with treatment of diseases?
questions.explanation: The Chikitsasthana is the section of the Charaka Samhita devoted mainly to therapeutics and the treatment of diseases.

questions.q: Which of these concepts is strongly emphasized in the Charaka Samhita as key to maintaining health?
options: Strict avoidance of all plant remedies
Balance of the doshas through diet and lifestyle
Reliance on surgery for most ailments
questions.explanation: Charaka stresses keeping the doshas in balance through diet, regimen and lifestyle as the basis of health.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita is notable for its early discussion of which preventive idea?
options: Maintaining the body's resistance to disease (the concept of immunity, bala/vyadhikshamatva)
questions.explanation: Charaka discusses the body's natural resistance to disease, an early conceptual treatment of what we would call immunity.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita was later translated into which language, helping spread Indian medicine westward in the medieval period?
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita was translated into Arabic and Persian, influencing medieval Islamic and later Western medicine.

questions.q: According to tradition associated with the Charaka Samhita, the science of Ayurveda was passed down beginning from which divine source?
questions.explanation: Ayurvedic tradition traces the knowledge to Brahma, transmitted through a lineage of gods and sages to human teachers.

questions.q: By tradition, Charaka is sometimes associated with the court of which dynasty's ruler?
questions.explanation: A later tradition links Charaka as a court physician to the Kushan emperor Kanishka, though this is not firmly established.

questions.q: In the Charaka Samhita's account of disease and treatment, which logical/diagnostic framework is emphasized?
options: Examination through means of knowledge such as perception, inference and authority
Astrological charts as the only diagnosis
questions.explanation: Charaka stresses systematic examination using pramanas like direct perception (pratyaksha), inference (anumana) and authoritative testimony.

questions.q: Which Charaka Samhita section, the Vimanasthana, is especially noted for discussing what?
options: Methods, measurement, ethics and a description of medical training/debate
questions.explanation: The Vimanasthana covers methodology, measurement, the ethics of practice and the training and conduct of physicians.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita classifies the basic 'tastes' (rasa) used in therapy and diet into how many?
questions.explanation: Ayurveda as in the Charaka Samhita recognizes six tastes (shad rasa): sweet, sour, salty, pungent, bitter and astringent.

questions.q: Charaka's text describes a fivefold purification therapy used to remove imbalances. What is this group called?
questions.explanation: Panchakarma is the set of five purification therapies described in classical Ayurveda for cleansing and rebalancing the body.

questions.q: The Charaka Samhita devotes a section to 'Rasayana'. What does Rasayana chiefly concern?
questions.explanation: Rasayana is the branch dealing with rejuvenation, promotion of longevity, strength and vitality.

questions.q: Which statement best reflects the Charaka Samhita's overall approach to medicine?
options: Surgery as the only valid therapy
Treatment based purely on supernatural exorcism
Medicine identical to modern germ theory
A holistic approach linking body, mind, diet and lifestyle
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita takes a holistic view, integrating diet, lifestyle, mind and the balance of doshas in health and treatment.

questions.q: The classical scholarly tradition holds that the Charaka Samhita reached its surviving form through a chain of authorship. Which order best reflects that tradition?
options: Agnivesha's compendium, redacted by Charaka, later restored by Dridhabala
Vagbhata alone composed the whole text
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Agnivesha's compendium was redacted by Charaka and later partly restored and completed by Dridhabala.
